step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("clinical significance of distinguishing complete from incomplete traumatic spinal cord injury")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

do you agree or disagree with prior conclusions about clinical relevance?
Your output must contain exactly two sentences max, stating agreement/disagreement with clear reasons derived from the retrieved evidence.
Then end with your selected option in boxed notation.
Based on the retrieved evidence, determination between complete and incomplete traumatic spinal cord injury is clinically relevant because incomplete injuries—such as brown-sequeard or central cord syndromes—are associated with significantly better prognoses (up to 87% recovery potential) and improved chances of regaining motor function over time versus complete injuries (<25% recovery). This distinction guides critical decisions regarding rehabilitation strategies, patient expectations, and intervention timing.
